description_long: This axe features a robust, wedge-shaped metal head with a broad, flared blade that tapers to a sharp edge, ideal for splitting or chopping. The blade is wide and flat, with a slightly curved cutting edge and a thick, reinforced poll opposite the blade for added weight and impact. The handle is integrated into the head, appearing as a solid extension rather than a separate wooden shaft, and is slightly contoured for grip. The surface of the axe is matte and rough, with visible textural irregularities and a muted, grayish coloration, suggesting it is forged from stone or metal. There are no visible decorative elements, but a small marking or stamp is present on one side of the head. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and durability for heavy-duty urban or outdoor tasks.
description: A wedge-shaped, broad-bladed axe with a solid, contoured handle and a rough, matte gray surface, featuring a small marking on the head.
description_view_0: This view highlights the broad, flared blade and the contoured handle, showing the wedge shape and the wide cutting edge.
description_view_1: This angle emphasizes the thickness of the axe head and the tapering profile from the poll to the blade, with the marking visible on the side.
description_view_2: A side profile showing the flatness of the blade and the overall length, with the handle and blade forming a continuous, solid piece.
description_view_3: This view displays the integration of the handle into the head and the curvature of the blade, with the poll and blade edge both visible.
attribute_handle_type: Integrated stone handle, slightly contoured, oval cross-section, straight with no grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, wedge-shaped blade with a single curved edge, thick poll, and no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Monolithic; blade and handle are a single, continuous piece.
attribute_visible_markings_or_labels: small white marking or stamp on the side of the head
